pages.name: Home
pages.description: Homepage with overview of boating how-to and safety content and quick access to all main sections.
primary_functions: Introduce the sites purpose and highlight key safety and how-to content.
Provide quick entry points to Safety Basics, How-To Guides, Checklists, Tools, Courses, and Glossary.
Allow users to start a site-wide search using the header search bar.
Surface featured beginner safety articles, key tools (e.g., capacity calculator), and common tasks.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Submit search form in the global header search bar
Click 'Safety Basics' in the header or a Safety Basics quick section card
Click 'How-To Guides' in the header or a how-to quick section card
Click 'Checklists' in the header or a checklist quick task
Click 'Tools' in the header or a featured tools shortcut
Click 'Courses' in the header or a training quick task
Click 'Glossary' in the header or a terminology quick link
Click 'My Learning Path' in the header or a learning path shortcut

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Home' link in the global header navigation
Users can land directly on the root URL, which resolves to index.html

pages.name: Safety Basics
pages.description: Listing page for safety-focused articles, especially beginner safety content, with rich filtering and sorting.
primary_functions: Browse safety articles in a list of cards showing title, read time, rating, and tags.
Filter articles by difficulty level (e.g., Beginner), read time (including a max read time slider/field), and rating (e.g., 4.0+ stars).
Sort articles by options such as Highest Rated, Newest First, and Shortest Read Time.
Add or remove articles from Favorites directly from article cards and open full article pages.

incoming_params.description: Optional difficulty filter preset, e.g., 'beginner', 'intermediate', 'advanced', 'all_levels'
Optional minimum rating filter, e.g., 4.0
Optional maximum read time in minutes used to position the read-time slider
Sort key matching available sort_options, e.g., 'highest_rated', 'newest_first', 'shortest_read_time'
Current results page number for pagination

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a safety article card title or 'Read More' link

access_methods.description: Primary access via 'Safety Basics' in the global header navigation
Filters and pagination can be deep-linked via URL query parameters

pages.name: Article
pages.description: Detail page for a single article or guide, including a print-friendly view.
primary_functions: Display full article content, including title, metadata (read time, rating, tags, boat size) and body text.
Provide a 'Print-Friendly View' mode that opens a simplified layout of the same article for printing.
Allow adding or removing the article from Favorites.
Show related articles or links back to the relevant listing (Safety Basics or How-To Guides).

incoming_params.description: ID of the Article to display
Optional view mode; when set to 'print', loads the print-friendly representation using getArticlePrintView

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'Print-Friendly View' button near the article title or share options

access_methods.description: Accessed via links that include the article ID as a query parameter
Articles can be shared/bookmarked directly with their ID and optional view mode in the URL

pages.name: Search Results
pages.description: Search results page for site-wide article and guide searches with filters and sorting.
primary_functions: Display results for a user-entered query from the header search bar (e.g., 'thunderstorm emergency').
Filter results by category (e.g., Safety Basics), boat size (e.g., Up to 26 ft), and date range.
Sort results by criteria such as Newest First or Highest Rated.
Open selected result items on the Article page for full reading and printing.

incoming_params.description: Search text entered into the global header search bar
Optional category filter, e.g., 'safety_basics'
Optional boat size filter, e.g., 'up_to_26_ft'
Optional ISO-8601 start date for date range filtering, e.g., '2022-01-01'
Optional ISO-8601 end date for date range filtering; often left blank
Sort key, e.g., 'newest_first', 'highest_rated'
Current page of search results

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a search result title or 'Open' link

access_methods.description: Accessed after submitting the global search form, which redirects here with the query parameter
Filters, sort, and pagination can be controlled through query parameters for deep linking

pages.name: How-To Guides
pages.description: Category-based listing of how-to guides for practical boating skills.
primary_functions: Browse how-to articles organized into category tabs such as Navigation, Anchoring & Mooring, and Communication & Electronics.
Scroll within each category list to see article cards with titles, tags (e.g., Beginner), and brief summaries.
Add or remove articles from the users Learning Path using an 'Add to Learning Path' icon on article cards.
Provide navigation (tabs and/or breadcrumb) to move between categories and back to the overall How-To listing.

incoming_params.description: Key of the selected category tab, e.g., 'navigation', 'anchoring_mooring', 'communication_electronics'
Current page for the selected category list

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a how-to article card title or 'Read Guide' link
Click 'My Learning Path' link in header or contextual CTA after adding articles

access_methods.description: Accessed via 'How-To Guides' in the global header navigation
Specific category tab and page can be addressed via query parameters

pages.name: My Learning Path
pages.description: Overview of the users custom learning path containing selected how-to articles.
primary_functions: Display the list of articles the user has added as steps in their personal learning path (e.g., night navigation, anchoring, VHF).
Show step order, category, and basic metadata (estimated time, difficulty) for each learning step.
Allow users to reorder or remove items from the learning path (e.g., move up/down, delete).
Provide direct links to open each learning path article on the Article or How-To Guides pages.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a learning path step title to open the full article
Click a 'Browse more guides' link to expand the learning path

access_methods.description: Accessed via 'My Learning Path' in the global header navigation
Users can bookmark learning_path.html to revisit their saved path

pages.name: Checklists
pages.description: Listing of boating safety and trip-preparation checklists with filters.
primary_functions: List available checklists with titles and brief descriptions (e.g., 'Pontoon Day Trip Safety Checklist').
Filter checklists by boat type (e.g., Pontoon), trip length (e.g., Day Trip 48 hours), and passenger range (e.g., 46 people).
Open a selected checklist on the Checklist Detail page for completion and saving.
Highlight popular or recommended safety checklists for frequent scenarios.

incoming_params.description: Optional boat type filter, e.g., 'pontoon'
Optional trip length filter, e.g., 'day_trip_4_8_hours'
Optional passenger range filter, e.g., 'passengers_4_6'
Current page number in the checklist listing

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a checklist title or 'Open Checklist' button

access_methods.description: Accessed via 'Checklists' in the global header navigation
Filter and pagination state can be encoded in URL parameters

pages.name: Checklist Detail
pages.description: Interactive page for viewing, checking off, and saving a specific checklist.
primary_functions: Display the full checklist broken into sections (e.g., 'Before You Leave Dock'), each with multiple items.
Allow users to scroll and mark individual checklist items as completed via checkboxes.
Provide a prominent 'Save Checklist' button to store completion progress for later reference on the same device/browser.
Show saved/unsaved status and last saved time for the current checklist.

incoming_params.description: ID of the Checklist to load and display

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Back to all checklists' breadcrumb or link

access_methods.description: Checklist is selected via the 'id' query parameter
Specific checklists can be shared/bookmarked with their ID

pages.name: Tools
pages.description: Hub page listing available boating tools and utilities.
primary_functions: Show tool cards or links such as 'Boat Capacity Calculator' and 'Engine Maintenance Scheduler' with short descriptions.
Navigate users to individual tool pages for more detailed interactions.
Highlight recommended tools for beginners and common safety planning tasks.
Provide contextual help describing how each tool supports safe boating decisions.

incoming_params.description: Optional tool slug to visually highlight, e.g., 'boat_capacity_calculator' or 'engine_maintenance_scheduler'

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'Boat Capacity Calculator' tool card
Click the 'Engine Maintenance Scheduler' tool card

access_methods.description: Accessed via 'Tools' in the global header navigation
Linked from Home and other pages when tools are referenced

pages.name: Boat Capacity Calculator
pages.description: Tool for calculating recommended maximum passenger capacity based on boat characteristics.
primary_functions: Collect input fields including boat length (feet), width/beam (feet), boat type (e.g., Open motorboat), and water type (e.g., Inland lake/river).
Calculate and display the recommended maximum passenger capacity based on user inputs.
Provide an 'Add to Trip Plan' action that opens a small form to capture a trip name (e.g., 'Weekend Lake Trip for 6') and save the plan.
Show confirmation that the trip plan with its capacity constraint has been saved for future reference.

incoming_params.description: Optional pre-filled boat length in feet
Optional pre-filled boat width/beam in feet
Optional pre-selected boat type, e.g., 'open_motorboat'
Optional pre-selected water type, e.g., 'inland_lake_river'

access_methods.description: Accessed from the Tools hub or call-to-action banners on other pages
Form fields can be pre-populated from URL parameters when linking from elsewhere

pages.name: Engine Maintenance Scheduler
pages.description: Tool for generating and saving a recurring engine maintenance schedule.
primary_functions: Collect scheduling inputs such as engine type (e.g., 2-stroke outboard), usage frequency (e.g., used monthly), water type (e.g., saltwater), start date, and reminder frequency.
Generate a maintenance schedule outlining key tasks and dates based on the provided inputs.
Display the generated schedule clearly, grouped by upcoming months or intervals.
Allow users to save the generated schedule so it is stored in the sites saved tools area for the current browser/device.

incoming_params.description: Optional pre-selected engine type, e.g., 'two_stroke_outboard'
Optional pre-selected usage frequency, e.g., 'used_monthly'
Optional pre-selected water type, e.g., 'saltwater'
Optional ISO-8601 start date for the schedule, e.g., '2024-07-01'
Optional pre-selected reminder frequency, e.g., 'every_1_month'

access_methods.description: Accessed from the Tools hub and from contextual prompts in engine-related content
Form options can be seeded via URL parameters for common presets

pages.name: Courses
pages.description: Directory of boating safety and skills courses with filters to find local, affordable options.
primary_functions: List available courses with essential information such as title, price, level (e.g., Beginner), and distance.
Filter courses by course type (e.g., Basic Boating Safety), distance (with a numeric field and units, e.g., miles), and maximum price.
Sort courses by options such as Price: Low to High, Rating, or Soonest Start Date.
Open a course on the Course Detail page to see more information and add it to the users training plan.

incoming_params.description: Optional course type filter, e.g., 'basic_boating_safety'
Maximum allowed course price in dollars, e.g., 100
Maximum distance filter value, e.g., 25
Distance unit, e.g., 'miles' or 'kilometers'
Sort key for courses, e.g., 'price_low_to_high'
Current page number in the course results

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a course title or 'View Details' link

access_methods.description: Accessed via 'Courses' in the global header navigation
Filters and sorting can be controlled by query parameters for shareable views

pages.name: Course Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a single course with ability to add it to the users training plan.
primary_functions: Show detailed course information including description, schedule pattern, provider, exact price, level, and location.
Provide an 'Add to My Training Plan' button that opens a small form or popup.
Allow the user to choose a planned start month (e.g., June 2024) and save the course into their training plan.
Confirm that the course has been saved and provide a link or hint for where the plan can be reviewed within the site.

incoming_params.description: ID of the Course to display

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Back to all courses' breadcrumb or link

access_methods.description: Accessed with a course ID in the URL
Course detail URLs can be shared/bookmarked

pages.name: Glossary & Study List
pages.description: Boating and radio terminology glossary with an integrated study-list view.
primary_functions: Provide a glossary search box where users can type terms (e.g., 'mayday', 'pan-pan', 'securite') and view matching entries.
Show glossary entries with definitions and an 'Add to Study List' icon for saving key terms.
Offer a 'My Study List' tab or link near the top that switches the view to display only saved terms, while using the same page file.
Allow basic management of the study list, such as reviewing saved terms and optionally removing them.

incoming_params.description: Search text pre-filled in the glossary search box
Determines whether to show the main glossary ('glossary') or the study list ('study_list')

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'My Study List' tab or link

access_methods.description: Accessed via 'Glossary' in the global header navigation
Study list view is toggled via a 'view' query parameter

pages.name: About
pages.description: Information about the purpose and scope of the boating how-to and safety blog.
primary_functions: Explain the sites mission, focus on safety, and target audience (e.g., new recreational boaters).
Describe the types of content available: articles, checklists, tools, and courses.
Provide background on content sources and any safety guidelines or standards followed.
Offer links back to key areas of the site for users who arrive here first.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a contextual link to start with Safety Basics articles
Click a link to explore How-To Guides
Click a link to view safety and trip-preparation checklists
Click a link highlighting planning tools
Click a link that guides users to formal boating courses
Click a link suggesting learning key boating terms

access_methods.description: Accessed via 'About' in the global footer
Can be accessed directly from external links describing the site

pages.name: Contact
pages.description: Contact and feedback page for the site.
primary_functions: Provide a simple contact form for submitting questions, feedback, or suggestions about the sites content and tools.
Display non-form contact information as appropriate (e.g., email address or mailing address).
Explain typical response expectations or usage of submitted feedback.
Link back to main sections so users can easily continue browsing after contacting.

incoming_params.description: Optional pre-selected topic for the contact form, e.g., 'content_feedback', 'tool_issue'

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Return to Home' link after successfully submitting the contact form

access_methods.description: Accessed via 'Contact' in the global footer
The contact form on this page submits via submitContactForm without a full page change
Contact page URL may be linked from external channels

header_links.description: Homepage with featured safety content and quick links
Safety articles and guides with filters
Step-by-step boating how-to content by category
Boating and radio terminology glossary and study list
View and manage your custom learning path
